codeReview.summary: The export targets are mostly wired up, but the implementation still misses three contract-level requirements: shared-body reuse, XML-safe claude/hermes envelopes, and locale-stable deterministic summaries.

issues.description: The exporter dispatches to separate per-target renderers instead of reusing `buildSharedBody`, so the canonical payload is reserialized differently for each target. That breaks the shared-body contract and makes byte-for-byte equivalence across targets impossible.

Claude/Hermes insert raw `session.bodyText` (and the surrounding key-file/key-command strings) directly inside `<context_pack>` without XML escaping or CDATA. Any `<`, `&`, or `</context_pack>` in content can invalidate the envelope and violates VAL-CTXEXP-006.

`buildUsageSummary` uses `.medium` date formatting without pinning `locale = en_US_POSIX`, so the exported summary text changes with host locale. That defeats the locale/timezone-stable determinism required by VAL-CTXEXP-007.

summary: Fail: the feature adds the new exporter/tests, but the contract still breaks on shared-body reuse, XML safety for claude/hermes, and deterministic locale-stable output.
